Panama.name: Panama
Panama.rivals: Costa Rica is Panama's fiercest football rivalry, while matches against the United States and Honduras have become increasingly significant in modern CONCACAF competition.

timeline.event: Reach their first Gold Cup final.
Agonisingly miss World Cup qualification after conceding two late goals against the United States.
Qualify for their first FIFA World Cup.
Reach the CONCACAF Nations League final.
Secure qualification for the 2026 World Cup.
Appear at their second World Cup.

players.name: Adalberto Carrasquilla
players.club: midfield leader and one of CONCACAF's most influential players.

players.name: José Fajardo
players.club: proven goalscorer in regional competition.

players.name: Michael Murillo
players.club: attacking full-back with major tournament experience.

Paraguay.name: Paraguay
Paraguay.rivals: Argentina is Paraguay's most historic football rival, while clashes with Uruguay and Brazil have frequently shaped World Cup qualification campaigns.

timeline.event: Participate in the inaugural FIFA World Cup.
Reach the Round of 16, narrowly losing to eventual champions France.
Reach the World Cup quarter-finals for the first time.
Reach the Copa América final without winning a match in regulation time.
Qualify for the World Cup after missing the previous three editions.
Return to the tournament for the first time since South Africa 2010.

players.name: Julio Enciso
players.club: Chelsea **[VERIFY club]**, creative star and face of the new generation.

players.name: Miguel Almirón
players.club: experienced attacking leader.

players.name: Diego Gómez
players.club: dynamic midfielder attracting growing international attention.

Portugal.name: Portugal
Portugal.rivals: Spain is Portugal's defining football rivalry, producing some of the most anticipated fixtures in international football. Historic encounters with France have also shaped the country's modern football story.

timeline.event: Reach the World Cup semi-finals on their debut, inspired by Eusébio.
Reach the European Championship final on home soil.
Reach a second World Cup semi-final.
Win the UEFA European Championship, the first major trophy in their history.
Win the inaugural UEFA Nations League.
Compete in what could be Cristiano Ronaldo's final World Cup.

players.name: Cristiano Ronaldo
players.club: one of the greatest footballers in history and still capable of deciding major matches.

players.name: Bruno Fernandes
players.club: the creative leader who dictates Portugal's attacking rhythm.

players.name: Vitinha
players.club: elegant midfielder increasingly viewed as one of the world's best in his position.

Qatar.name: Qatar
Qatar.rivals: Saudi Arabia remains Qatar's most significant football rival, while encounters with the UAE have often carried extra intensity within Gulf football.

timeline.event: Qatar Football Association is established.
Wins its first Gulf Cup.
Wins the AFC Asian Cup for the first time, defeating Japan 3-1 in the final.
Hosts the FIFA World Cup, becoming the first Arab nation to do so.
Qualifies for the 2026 World Cup through AFC qualifying.
Competes in its second consecutive World Cup.

players.name: Akram Afif
players.club: arguably the greatest player in Qatari football history.

players.name: Almoez Ali
players.club: all-time leading goalscorer and Asian Cup hero.

players.name: Lucas Mendes
players.club: defensive leader and cornerstone of the back line.

Saudi Arabia.name: Saudi Arabia
Saudi Arabia.rivals: Iran is Saudi Arabia's defining football rivalry, while matches against Qatar and Iraq consistently rank among the region's most anticipated fixtures.

timeline.event: Wins its first AFC Asian Cup.
Reaches the Round of 16 at its World Cup debut.
Reaches the AFC Asian Cup final.
Defeats Argentina 2-1 in one of World Cup history's biggest shocks.
Secures qualification for a seventh World Cup appearance.
Returns as one of Asia's most experienced tournament nations.

players.name: Salem Al-Dawsari
players.club: scorer of the winning goal against Argentina and national icon.

players.name: Musab Al-Juwayr
players.club: highly regarded midfielder representing the next generation.

players.name: Hassan Kadesh
players.club: experienced defensive leader.

Scotland.name: Scotland
Scotland.rivals: England is Scotland's defining football rivalry and the oldest international fixture in world football. Encounters with Ireland also carry significant historical importance.

timeline.event: Plays England in the first official international football match.
Goes unbeaten at the World Cup but is eliminated on goal difference.
Arrives in Argentina amid huge expectations before suffering a disappointing exit.
Makes its most recent World Cup appearance prior to 2026.
Returns to a major tournament after a 23-year absence by qualifying for Euro 2020.
Qualifies for its first World Cup in 28 years.

players.name: Scott McTominay
players.club: midfield leader and one of Scotland's most important goalscorers.

players.name: Andrew Robertson
players.club: captain and emotional heartbeat of the side.

players.name: Billy Gilmour
players.club: creative midfielder capable of controlling the tempo of matches.

Senegal.name: Senegal
Senegal.rivals: Morocco and Algeria have emerged as Senegal's principal continental rivals, while clashes with Côte d'Ivoire consistently rank among Africa's biggest fixtures.

timeline.event: Reach the World Cup quarter-finals on their debut, defeating defending champions France in the opening match.
Reach the Africa Cup of Nations final.
Win the Africa Cup of Nations for the first time.
Reach the World Cup Round of 16.
Qualify comfortably for the 2026 World Cup.
Arrive as one of Africa's strongest contenders.

players.name: Nicolas Jackson
players.club: dynamic forward capable of stretching any defence.

players.name: Pape Matar Sarr
players.club: midfield engine entering his prime years.

players.name: Kalidou Koulibaly
players.club: captain and defensive leader with vast tournament experience.

South Africa.name: South Africa
South Africa.rivals: Nigeria is South Africa's most significant modern football rivalry, while encounters with Zimbabwe carry additional regional importance in Southern Africa.

timeline.event: Wins the Africa Cup of Nations on home soil.
Makes its World Cup debut in France.
Hosts the first FIFA World Cup held in Africa.
Finishes third at the Africa Cup of Nations, their best result in over two decades.
Qualifies for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
Returns to the World Cup after a long absence.

players.name: Ronwen Williams
players.club: captain and one of Africa's most accomplished goalkeepers.

players.name: Teboho Mokoena
players.club: midfield leader and set-piece specialist.

players.name: Relebohile Mofokeng
players.club: exciting young attacker widely viewed as the future of South African football.
